**Karol Tadeusz Nawrocki*[[a]](#cite_note-1)*** (born 3 March 1983) is a Polish historian and politician who has served as the 7th [president of Poland](/wiki/President_of_Poland "President of Poland") since 2025. Previously, he served as the director of the [Institute of National Remembrance](/wiki/Institute_of_National_Remembrance "Institute of National Remembrance") (IPN) from 2021 to 2025, and was the director of the [Museum of the Second World War](/wiki/Museum_of_the_Second_World_War "Museum of the Second World War") in [Gdańsk](/wiki/Gda%C5%84sk "Gdańsk") from 2017 to 2021.

Born in Gdańsk, Nawrocki studied history at the [University of Gdańsk](/wiki/University_of_Gda%C5%84sk "University of Gdańsk"), earning a [PhD](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy") in 2013 with a dissertation on [anti-communist](/wiki/Anti-communist "Anti-communist") activities in the former [Polish People's Republic](/wiki/Polish_People%27s_Republic "Polish People's Republic"). His academic work centers on themes such as [anti-communist resistance in Poland](/wiki/Anti-communist_resistance_in_Poland_(1944%E2%80%931989) "Anti-communist resistance in Poland (1944–1989)"), [organized crime](/wiki/Organized_crime "Organized crime"), and the [history of sports](/wiki/History_of_sports "History of sports"), a subject related to his own background as an active youth athlete, particularly in [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"). Nawrocki's early professional career was closely aligned with institutions dedicated to preserving and promoting Poland's historical memory. He joined the Institute of National Remembrance (IPN) in 2009 and gained recognition for reorienting Poland's historical institutions toward a patriotic and anti-communist narrative. In the [2025 presidential election](/wiki/2025_Polish_presidential_election "2025 Polish presidential election"), Nawrocki was chosen as the presidential candidate of the right-wing [Law and Justice](/wiki/Law_and_Justice "Law and Justice") (PiS) party, ran as a nonpartisan "citizens' candidate" and defeated the liberal [Rafał Trzaskowski](/wiki/Rafa%C5%82_Trzaskowski "Rafał Trzaskowski").

As president, he has reflected a broader rightward shift in Poland's political landscape. Since his inauguration on 6 August 2025, Nawrocki has been following a confrontational course with the [ruling coalition](/wiki/15_October_Coalition "15 October Coalition") and professed opposition to its leader, [Donald Tusk](/wiki/Donald_Tusk "Donald Tusk"). Nawrocki strives to strengthen power around the presidency, supporting [constitutional reform](/wiki/Constitutional_reform "Constitutional reform") into a [semi-presidential system](/wiki/Semi-presidential_system "Semi-presidential system"). His [chancellery](/wiki/Chancellery_of_the_President_of_the_Republic_of_Poland "Chancellery of the President of the Republic of Poland") is made up of PiS politicians and his IPN affiliates.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Karol Nawrocki was born on 3 March 1983, in [Gdańsk](/wiki/Gda%C5%84sk "Gdańsk"). His father, Ryszard (1949–2008), was a turner and a member of [Solidarity](/wiki/Solidarity_(Polish_trade_union) "Solidarity (Polish trade union)"). His mother, Elżbieta, was a bookbinder. He has a younger sister named Nina who is a pastry cook and works in a restaurant in Kraków. He completed primary school and the 4th High School in his home city.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) After passing his final exams ([matura](/wiki/Matura "Matura")) in 2002, he graduated in 2003 from the Post-Secondary School of Business and Administration in Gdańsk with the title of Personnel Management Specialist.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) In the same year, he began his university studies at Faculty of History at the [University of Gdańsk](/wiki/University_of_Gda%C5%84sk "University of Gdańsk"), which he completed in 2008 with a master's degree.[[2]](#cite_note-3)

At the same institute, he obtained a PhD in the humanities based on his dissertation titled: *Social Resistance to Communist Rule in the Elbląg Voivodeship, 1976–1989*.[[3]](#cite_note-ipn-pres-4)[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) In 2023, he completed the International MBA in Strategy, Programme and Project Management postgraduate studies at the [Gdańsk University of Technology](/wiki/Gda%C5%84sk_University_of_Technology "Gdańsk University of Technology").[[4]](#cite_note-5) In his youth, Nawrocki developed a strong interest in physical fitness, spending time working out at the gym, and participating in [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing").[[5]](#cite_note-6)

## Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/Second_World_War_Museum%27s_representatives_visit_New_Zealand_5.jpg/250px-Second_World_War_Museum%27s_representatives_visit_New_Zealand_5.jpg)](/wiki/File:Second_World_War_Museum%27s_representatives_visit_New_Zealand_5.jpg)

Nawrocki as the director of the Museum of the Second World War during a meeting with [Polish diaspora](/wiki/Polish_diaspora "Polish diaspora") in [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"), February 2020

Nawrocki worked at the Institute of National Remembrance in the years 2009–2017, heading its Branch Public Education Office in Gdańsk from 2013 to 2017. He also served as the chairman of the [Siedlce](/wiki/Siedlce,_Gda%C5%84sk "Siedlce, Gdańsk") District Council in Gdańsk between 2011 and 2017.[[3]](#cite_note-ipn-pres-4)

In 2017, Nawrocki was appointed the director of the [Museum of the Second World War in Gdańsk](/wiki/Museum_of_the_Second_World_War "Museum of the Second World War"),[[6]](#cite_note-7) a job he held until 2021. He then returned to the Institute of National Remembrance, becoming its deputy president in June 2021.[[7]](#cite_note-8) In July 2021, he took office as the head of the Institute of National Remembrance after being elected by the [Sejm](/wiki/Sejm "Sejm") and approved by the [Senate of Poland](/wiki/Senate_of_Poland "Senate of Poland").[[8]](#cite_note-9) Nawrocki is the author or co-author of several books as well as numerous scientific and popular science papers on anti-communist opposition, organised crime in the [Polish People's Republic](/wiki/Polish_People%27s_Republic "Polish People's Republic") and the history of sports.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Nawrocki used the [pen name](/wiki/Pen_name "Pen name") "Tadeusz Batyr" to write a book about a gangster living in 1980s [Communist Poland](/wiki/Communist_Poland "Communist Poland").[[9]](#cite_note-Vernon-10) Nawrocki in 2018 went on [television](/wiki/Television_in_Poland "Television in Poland") as Tadeusz Batyr, wearing a hat and having the television broadcast blur his face, where he said that Nawrocki "inspired me" and highlighted how Nawrocki "was the first person to examine organised crime in communist Poland".[[9]](#cite_note-Vernon-10) Meanwhile, on social media, Nawrocki wrote that "Tadeusz Batyr contacted me for some guidance" and "thanked me for my help with an interesting book, which I recommend".[[9]](#cite_note-Vernon-10)

### Rise to candidacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Rise to candidacy")]

Nawrocki was in consideration among speculative candidates such as PiS parliamentary leader [Mariusz Błaszczak](/wiki/Mariusz_B%C5%82aszczak "Mariusz Błaszczak"), [MEP](/wiki/Member_of_the_European_Parliament "Member of the European Parliament") [Tobiasz Bocheński](/wiki/Tobiasz_Boche%C5%84ski "Tobiasz Bocheński"), [poseł](/wiki/Sejm "Sejm") [Zbigniew Bogucki](/wiki/Zbigniew_Bogucki "Zbigniew Bogucki"), former [Minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_National_Education_(Poland) "Ministry of National Education (Poland)") [Przemysław Czarnek](/wiki/Przemys%C5%82aw_Czarnek "Przemysław Czarnek"), MEP [Patryk Jaki](/wiki/Patryk_Jaki "Patryk Jaki"), former [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Poland "Prime Minister of Poland") [Mateusz Morawiecki](/wiki/Mateusz_Morawiecki "Mateusz Morawiecki"), poseł [Arkadiusz Mularczyk](/wiki/Arkadiusz_Mularczyk "Arkadiusz Mularczyk"), poseł [Kacper Płażyński](/wiki/Kacper_P%C5%82a%C5%BCy%C5%84ski "Kacper Płażyński"), former [Deputy Minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_National_Defence_(Poland) "Ministry of National Defence (Poland)") [Tomasz Szatkowski](/wiki/Tomasz_Szatkowski "Tomasz Szatkowski") and MEP [Dominik Tarczyński](/wiki/Dominik_Tarczy%C5%84ski "Dominik Tarczyński"),[[10]](#cite_note-11)[[11]](#cite_note-12) among others. President of the [World Anti-Doping Agency](/wiki/World_Anti-Doping_Agency "World Anti-Doping Agency"), [Witold Bańka](/wiki/Witold_Ba%C5%84ka "Witold Bańka"), was also considered Kaczyński's favoured candidate, but he refused to run in the election.[[12]](#cite_note-13)[[13]](#cite_note-14)

Because of the centralized nature of the party, the choice for selecting who the party's candidate would be fell primarily to the party chairman, [Jarosław Kaczyński](/wiki/Jaros%C5%82aw_Kaczy%C5%84ski "Jarosław Kaczyński"), based on social research[[14]](#cite_note-15) and the balance of factional power within the party.[[15]](#cite_note-16)[[16]](#cite_note-17) In an August [Radio Maryja](/wiki/Radio_Maryja "Radio Maryja") interview, Kaczyński stated that the selection of possible candidates was narrowing, and described that the PiS candidate must be a "young, tall, imposing, handsome" male "[with] a family".[[17]](#cite_note-18)[[18]](#cite_note-19) A United Surveys poll in September 2024 suggested 29% of PiS voters supported Błaszczak, 21% supported Morawiecki, 11% supported Czarnek, 7% supported Nawrocki, 6% supported Bocheński and 6% supported Tarczyński.[[19]](#cite_note-20) The possibility of a primary election for the party was rumored, but ultimately, never announced.[[20]](#cite_note-21)

By November 2024, media speculation regarding the candidates narrowed down to, most frequently, Bocheński, Czarnek and Nawrocki.[[21]](#cite_note-22) According to *[Newsweek](/wiki/Newsweek_Polska "Newsweek Polska")*, Nawrocki was supported internally by party activists such as [Adam Bielan](/wiki/Adam_Bielan "Adam Bielan"), [Joachim Brudziński](/wiki/Joachim_Brudzi%C5%84ski "Joachim Brudziński"), [Sławomir Cenckiewicz](/wiki/S%C5%82awomir_Cenckiewicz "Sławomir Cenckiewicz"), [Marta Kaczyńska](/w/index.php?title=Marta_Kaczy%C5%84ska&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marta Kaczyńska (page does not exist)") [[pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marta_Kaczy%C5%84ska "pl:Marta Kaczyńska")], and Mateusz Morawiecki (despite initially desiring to himself be the candidate).[[22]](#cite_note-newsweekkulisy-23) Ultimately, at 13.15 on 22 November, Nawrocki was informed that he had been selected as the party's presidential candidate in a meeting with Kaczyński and his opponent, Bocheński.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

The selection of Nawrocki without consultation with outgoing president [Andrzej Duda](/wiki/Andrzej_Duda "Andrzej Duda") (the previous PiS candidate) strained relations between him and Nawrocki,[[24]](#cite_note-25) although Duda eventually endorsed Nawrocki at his election convention in [Łódź](/wiki/%C5%81%C3%B3d%C5%BA "Łódź") on 26 April.[[25]](#cite_note-26)

### First round of the presidential election

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: First round of the presidential election")]

On 24 November 2024, at the [Sokół Gymnastics Society Building](/w/index.php?title=Sok%C3%B3%C5%82_Gymnastics_Society_Building&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sokół Gymnastics Society Building (page does not exist)") [[pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Budynek_Towarzystwa_Gimnastycznego_%E2%80%9ESok%C3%B3%C5%82%E2%80%9D_w_Krakowie "pl:Budynek Towarzystwa Gimnastycznego „Sokół” w Krakowie")], during a civic congress organised by [Law and Justice](/wiki/Law_and_Justice "Law and Justice"), Nawrocki was announced by [Andrzej Nowak](/wiki/Andrzej_Nowak_(historian) "Andrzej Nowak (historian)") as an [independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") candidate supported by the party in the [2025 presidential election](/wiki/2025_Polish_presidential_election "2025 Polish presidential election").[[26]](#cite_note-27)[[27]](#cite_note-28)[[28]](#cite_note-29) Nawrocki's campaign's platform was described as "patriotic, pro-Christian, pro-NATO, pro-Western, and pro-Donald Trump".[[29]](#cite_note-:0-30) On 1 May 2025, Nawrocki met with [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") in the [Oval Office](/wiki/Oval_Office "Oval Office").[[30]](#cite_note-31)[[31]](#cite_note-32) He finished second in the first round held on 18 May 2025 with 29.54% of the vote, and faced [Civic Platform](/wiki/Civic_Platform "Civic Platform") candidate [Rafał Trzaskowski](/wiki/Rafa%C5%82_Trzaskowski "Rafał Trzaskowski") in a runoff on 1 June.[[32]](#cite_note-33)

### Second round of the presidential election

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Second round of the presidential election")]

On 22 May 2025, Nawrocki met with [Sławomir Mentzen](/wiki/S%C5%82awomir_Mentzen "Sławomir Mentzen"), who placed third in the first round of the election. They reportedly discussed possible concessions that could lead to Mentzen endorsing Nawrocki.[[33]](#cite_note-:4-34) Mentzen presented a set of eight points[[b]](#cite_note-35) for a candidate to sign onto,[[34]](#cite_note-36) which were signed by Nawrocki.[[35]](#cite_note-37)[[36]](#cite_note-38)[[37]](#cite_note-39) Former candidates [Marek Jakubiak](/wiki/Marek_Jakubiak "Marek Jakubiak"), [Marek Woch](/wiki/Marek_Woch "Marek Woch"), [Grzegorz Braun](/wiki/Grzegorz_Braun "Grzegorz Braun") and [Artur Bartoszewicz](/wiki/Artur_Bartoszewicz "Artur Bartoszewicz") endorsed Nawrocki in the [runoff](/wiki/Two-round_system "Two-round system").[[38]](#cite_note-40)[[39]](#cite_note-41)[[40]](#cite_note-braunpoparcie-42)[[41]](#cite_note-43) On 1 June 2025, Nawrocki won the runoff election with 50.9% of votes, beating Trzaskowski, who received 49.1% of votes.[[42]](#cite_note-44)[[43]](#cite_note-45)

### Controversies

Nawrocki was the subject of various affairs during the [2025 presidential election](/wiki/2025_Polish_presidential_election "2025 Polish presidential election"). Speculation began on whether PiS would replace Nawrocki when it was revealed that he had contact with a future criminal during his time as a boxer two decades prior, for which he was attacked by opposing politicians.[[44]](#cite_note-46) Polling showed, however, that the vast plurality of people did not expect Nawrocki to be replaced.[[45]](#cite_note-47) Nawrocki would continue being PiS' presidential candidate going into the first round and would face further controversies about his private life.

During the course of the campaign, public criticism emerged over Nawrocki's acquisition of a second apartment from an elderly man in pre-trial detention. As Nawrocki had declared to own just one apartment during a debate, [Onet.pl](/wiki/Onet.pl "Onet.pl") publicised information about him owning a second one. The candidate proceeded to declare that he had acquired the second apartment from the elderly man for pledging lifelong care in exchange; however, it was revealed the man was placed in a state care facility without Nawrocki's involvement.[[46]](#cite_note-48) Amid accusations of exploitation, Nawrocki defended the deal's legality and donated the property to charity, after which some journalists claimed the man regained access to the property. He also stated he had regularly tried to visit the elderly man, but without success.[[47]](#cite_note-49)

Throughout the second round, Nawrocki encountered a set of new controversies involving his personal life, beginning with the revelation that he had previously participated in a 70 vs 70 [football hooligans' fight](/wiki/Football_hooliganism_in_Poland "Football hooliganism in Poland") ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *ustawka*) between fans of [Lechia Gdańsk](/wiki/Lechia_Gda%C5%84sk "Lechia Gdańsk") and [Lech Poznań](/wiki/Lech_Pozna%C5%84 "Lech Poznań").[[48]](#cite_note-50) Facing criticism, he said that Prime Minister Tusk likewise participated in football hooliganism in his youth, and called the fights "noble battles".[[49]](#cite_note-51)[[50]](#cite_note-Gądek-52) It also came to light that Nawrocki had [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") and Lechia Gdańsk tattoos on his torso.[[51]](#cite_note-53) Further controversies about Nawrocki's personal background emerged due to allegations of him having worked as a bodyguard for prostitutes at the five-star [Grand Hotel](/wiki/Grand_Hotel,_Sopot "Grand Hotel, Sopot") in [Sopot](/wiki/Sopot "Sopot") in his youth[[52]](#cite_note-54) and using a [nicotine pouch](/wiki/Nicotine_pouch "Nicotine pouch") while on air during a presidential debate,[[53]](#cite_note-55) which raised concerns about a possible nicotine addiction.[[54]](#cite_note-56)[[55]](#cite_note-57)

## President of Poland (2025–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: President of Poland (2025–present)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/05/Karol_Nawrocki_speaking_after_being_sworn_in_on_6_August_2025.jpg/330px-Karol_Nawrocki_speaking_after_being_sworn_in_on_6_August_2025.jpg)](/wiki/File:Karol_Nawrocki_speaking_after_being_sworn_in_on_6_August_2025.jpg)

Karol Nawrocki giving a speech at his inauguration on 6 August 2025

Nawrocki was inaugurated as president of Poland on 6 August 2025, succeeding [Andrzej Duda](/wiki/Andrzej_Duda "Andrzej Duda"). On the same day he participated in the ceremony of accepting the authority of the President of Poland on the [Polish Armed Forces](/wiki/Polish_Armed_Forces "Polish Armed Forces").[[56]](#cite_note-58)

### Domestic policy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Domestic policy")]

#### Relations with the Prime Minister and government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Relations with the Prime Minister and government")]

The first months of Nawrocki's presidency were marked by a confrontational stance against [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Poland "Prime Minister of Poland") [Donald Tusk](/wiki/Donald_Tusk "Donald Tusk") and his [ruling coalition](/wiki/Third_Tusk_cabinet "Third Tusk cabinet"),[[57]](#cite_note-59) citing his large and recent [social mandate](/wiki/Mandate_(politics) "Mandate (politics)") after the recent presidential election.[[58]](#cite_note-60) Nawrocki views Tusk as the worst Prime Minister of the [Third Polish Republic](/wiki/History_of_Poland_(1989%E2%80%93present) "History of Poland (1989–present)"), but expresses willingness to work with him regardless.[[59]](#cite_note-61) Analysts consider the downfall of Tusk's coalition and early [parliamentary elections](/wiki/Next_Polish_parliamentary_election "Next Polish parliamentary election") as a goal for Nawrocki,[[60]](#cite_note-62) with him also seen as competing with Tusk's government in matters of lawmaking and governance despite the president having a primarily representative role,[[61]](#cite_note-governance-63) announcing he is seeking a "new formula of cooperation" between the President and [parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Poland "Parliament of Poland").[[62]](#cite_note-64) Nawrocki held his first meeting with Tusk on 14 August,[[63]](#cite_note-65) and on 27 August convened the first [Cabinet Council](/w/index.php?title=Cabinet_Council&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cabinet Council (page does not exist)") [[pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rada_Gabinetowa "pl:Rada Gabinetowa")], oriented on finances, investments and agriculture.[[64]](#cite_note-66)

#### Partisan affiliations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Partisan affiliations")]

See also: [Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency § Will Nawrocki be/is Nawrocki an independent president?](/wiki/Opinion_polling_on_the_Karol_Nawrocki_presidency#Will_Nawrocki_be/is_Nawrocki_an_independent_president? "Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency")

Karol Nawrocki was elected primarily with support of the right-wing populist [Law and Justice](/wiki/Law_and_Justice "Law and Justice") (PiS) party and, despite being officially independent, was considered the PiS candidate in the [2025 presidential election](/wiki/2025_Polish_presidential_election "2025 Polish presidential election").[[65]](#cite_note-67) However, Nawrocki's affiliation as a "PiS president" is debated, with some suggesting he could be a more independent president, or could cooperate with the far-right [Confederation](/wiki/Confederation_Liberty_and_Independence "Confederation Liberty and Independence") as well, despite its [libertarian](/wiki/Economic_liberalism "Economic liberalism") economic policies conflicting with PiS.[[66]](#cite_note-68) Alongside seeking to empower his role in governance of the country[[61]](#cite_note-governance-63) and reform Poland into a [semi-presidential republic](/wiki/Semi-presidential_republic "Semi-presidential republic"),[[67]](#cite_note-Adamski-Konstytucja-69) Nawrocki is perceived as aspiring to lead the "right wing" of politics, possibly threatening the position of established political leaders such as [Jarosław Kaczyński](/wiki/Jaros%C5%82aw_Kaczy%C5%84ski "Jarosław Kaczyński").[[68]](#cite_note-70)

#### Constitutional reform

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Constitutional reform")]

See also: [Proposed Fourth Polish Republic](/wiki/Proposed_Fourth_Polish_Republic "Proposed Fourth Polish Republic") and [Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency § Constitutional reform](/wiki/Opinion_polling_on_the_Karol_Nawrocki_presidency#Constitutional_reform "Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency")

At his inaugural speech, Nawrocki announced intention to create a new [Constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Poland "Constitution of Poland") by 2030,[[69]](#cite_note-71) announcing the creation of a Council for the Reform of the System of the State[[70]](#cite_note-72) ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *Rada ds. Naprawy Ustroju Państwa*).[[71]](#cite_note-73) Soon after, Paweł Szefernaker, Nawrocki's appointed cabinet chief, elaborated that Nawrocki aims to empower the presidency.[[72]](#cite_note-74) According to [RMF FM](/wiki/RMF_FM "RMF FM"), the Council is to be chaired by presidential advisor [Dariusz Dudek](/w/index.php?title=Dariusz_Waldemar_Dudek&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dariusz Waldemar Dudek (page does not exist)"), and seek to reform, among others, the form of government from the current [parliamentary-cabinet](/w/index.php?title=Parliamentary-cabinet_system&action=edit&redlink=1 "Parliamentary-cabinet system (page does not exist)") [[pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/system_gabinetowo-parlametnarny "pl:system gabinetowo-parlametnarny")] to a [semi-presidential system](/wiki/Semi-presidential_system "Semi-presidential system").[[67]](#cite_note-Adamski-Konstytucja-69) In November 2025, Nawrocki stated that if the [government](/wiki/Third_Cabinet_of_Donald_Tusk "Third Cabinet of Donald Tusk") continued what he described as the "process of destabilization of Poland" by [non-recognition of judicial authorities](/wiki/Polish_constitutional_crisis "Polish constitutional crisis"), he would work to initiate a constitutional referendum in the subsequent year.[[73]](#cite_note-75)

#### Legislative initiatives and vetoes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Legislative initiatives and vetoes")]

See also: [Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency § Legislative proposals and other initiatives](/wiki/Opinion_polling_on_the_Karol_Nawrocki_presidency#Legislative_proposals_and_other_initiatives "Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7e/Kolbuszowa_%28plac_Wolno%C5%9Bci%29_-_spotkanie_z_prezydentem_Karolem_Nawrockim_11.jpg/250px-Kolbuszowa_%28plac_Wolno%C5%9Bci%29_-_spotkanie_z_prezydentem_Karolem_Nawrockim_11.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kolbuszowa_(plac_Wolno%C5%9Bci)_-_spotkanie_z_prezydentem_Karolem_Nawrockim_11.jpg)

Nawrocki signing the 0% PIT for families bill on 8 August 2025

Part of Nawrocki's conflict strategy against the Tusk cabinet is to use his [privilege as president](/wiki/Right_of_initiative_(legislative) "Right of initiative (legislative)") to introduce laws to parliament and to reshape coalition legislation by vetoing laws and re-submitting them with the disputed provisions removed.[[61]](#cite_note-governance-63) In the first weeks of Nawrocki's presidency, Nawrocki introduced legislature that would restore the old project of the [Central Communication Port](/wiki/Central_Communication_Port "Central Communication Port"),[[74]](#cite_note-76) abolish PIT for families with more than one child, increase the [income tax threshold](/wiki/Income_tax_threshold "Income tax threshold") to an annual income of 140,000 PLN,[[75]](#cite_note-77) extend the prohibition of sale of Polish land to foreigners.[[76]](#cite_note-78) On 21 August, Nawrocki for the first time used the presidential [veto](/wiki/Veto "Veto") against an [omnibus bill](/wiki/Omnibus_bill "Omnibus bill") regarding [wind turbine](/wiki/Wind_turbine "Wind turbine") deregulation and the extension of energy [price freeze](/wiki/Price_freeze "Price freeze") period, criticizing the deregulation as pushed by a wind turbine [lobby](/wiki/Lobbying "Lobbying") and unsafe, stating he would propose his own price freeze bill.[[77]](#cite_note-79)

On 3 November 2025, Nawrocki introduced a proposed bill titled "Retiring with Dignity", which would rise the minimum wage to 2,000 PLN (up from 1,878 PLN), and introduce the [indexation](/wiki/Indexation "Indexation") of social pensions both as a percentage and by a fixed amount, with a guaranteed increase of at least 150 PLN for lowest pensions.[[78]](#cite_note-80) Dual pension indexation was one of the Tusk's 100 pledges in the 2023 election.[[79]](#cite_note-81)

#### Historical policy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Historical policy")]

During his presidency, Nawrocki has been opposed to [fascist Ukrainian](/wiki/Far-right_politics_in_Ukraine "Far-right politics in Ukraine") symbolism and activities. Nawrocki pursued the criminalisation of [Banderite](/wiki/Banderite "Banderite") ideology in Poland, including the equalisation of Banderite symbols with [Nazi](/wiki/Nazism "Nazism") and [communist](/wiki/Communism "Communism") symbolism.[[80]](#cite_note-82) On 31 August 2025, the 45th anniversary of the [August Agreements](/wiki/Gda%C5%84sk_Agreement "Gdańsk Agreement") which created the [Solidarity](/wiki/Solidarity_(Polish_trade_union) "Solidarity (Polish trade union)") trade union, Nawrocki criticized former union leader and president [Lech Wałęsa](/wiki/Lech_Wa%C5%82%C4%99sa "Lech Wałęsa"), stating that while Wałęsa cannot be forgotten, he must be correctly and truthfully remembered.[[81]](#cite_note-83)

Nawrocki advocates for pursuing [World War II reparations](/wiki/World_War_II_reparations#Poland "World War II reparations") from [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), which Poland never received. On a WW2 anniversary on 1 September 2025, Nawrocki "unequivocally" demanded Germany pays Poland war reparations worth over 6 trillion [PLN](/wiki/Polish_z%C5%82oty "Polish złoty") (1.4 trillion [Euros](/wiki/Euro "Euro")),[[82]](#cite_note-84) continuing the course set by the [Law and Justice](/wiki/Law_and_Justice "Law and Justice") party.[[83]](#cite_note-85) During a visit to Germany in September 2025, Nawrocki attempted to discuss the topic of war reparations, but was rejected, with Germany stating that the matter of war reparations is "definitively regulated".[[84]](#cite_note-86) An alternative solution to paying war reparations proposed by Nawrocki was for Poland to receive financial aid for its military industry.[[85]](#cite_note-87)

Meeting between Karol Nawrocki and Ukrainian President [Volodymyr Zelenskyy](/wiki/Volodymyr_Zelenskyy "Volodymyr Zelenskyy") on 19 December 2025

According to analysts, the dispute over whether the President or Prime Minister should represent Poland at international meetings is an axis of conflict between Tusk and Nawrocki, titled as a "war for the seat".[[86]](#cite_note-88) On 13 August, Nawrocki represented Poland at a [teleconference](/wiki/Teleconference "Teleconference") of Trump and European leaders regarding the upcoming [2025 Russia–United States summit](/wiki/2025_Russia%E2%80%93United_States_summit "2025 Russia–United States summit") in Alaska, a change from Tusk previously representing the country in international conferences.[[87]](#cite_note-89) However, the lack of any Polish representation at an [18 August White house meeting](/wiki/August_2025_European-White_House_crisis_meeting "August 2025 European-White House crisis meeting") caused controversy, with some blaming Nawrocki for not representing the country at the meeting.[[88]](#cite_note-90)

During his presidency, Nawrocki attended many international meetings, and in September 2025, Nawrocki began travelling abroad. In September alone, he visited: 3 September — the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") for a meeting with [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump"), 5 September — a meeting with [Pope Leo XIV](/wiki/Pope_Leo_XIV "Pope Leo XIV") and [Italian Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Italy "Prime Minister of Italy") [Giorgia Meloni](/wiki/Giorgia_Meloni "Giorgia Meloni") and [President](/wiki/President_of_Italy "President of Italy") [Sergio Mattarella](/wiki/Sergio_Mattarella "Sergio Mattarella") in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome");[[89]](#cite_note-91) 8 September — in [Vilnius](/wiki/Vilnius "Vilnius") with [President of Lithuania](/wiki/President_of_Lithuania "President of Lithuania") [Gitanas Nauseda](/wiki/Gitanas_Nauseda "Gitanas Nauseda");[[90]](#cite_note-92) 9 September — in [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki") with [President of Finland](/wiki/President_of_Finland "President of Finland") [Alexander Stubb](/wiki/Alexander_Stubb "Alexander Stubb");[[91]](#cite_note-93) 16 September — a meeting in [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") with [Chancellor of Germany](/wiki/Chancellor_of_Germany "Chancellor of Germany") [Friedrich Merz](/wiki/Friedrich_Merz "Friedrich Merz") and [President](/wiki/President_of_Germany "President of Germany") [Frank-Walter Steinmeier](/wiki/Frank-Walter_Steinmeier "Frank-Walter Steinmeier");[[92]](#cite_note-94) and with [President of France](/wiki/President_of_France "President of France") [Emmanuel Macron](/wiki/Emmanuel_Macron "Emmanuel Macron") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris");[[93]](#cite_note-95) 21–24 September — a visit to the United States in [Doylestown, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Doylestown,_Pennsylvania "Doylestown, Pennsylvania"), to meet with the local [Polish diaspora](/wiki/Polish_diaspora "Polish diaspora"),[[94]](#cite_note-96) and [New York](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") for the 80th session of the [United Nations General Assembly](/wiki/United_Nations_General_Assembly "United Nations General Assembly").[[95]](#cite_note-97)

Soon after his inauguration, Nawrocki was invited to the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") by [President of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump").[[96]](#cite_note-98) With Nawrocki bringing no [Ministry of Foreign Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_of_Foreign_Affairs_(Poland) "Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Poland)") delegates to the meeting, foreign minister [Radosław Sikorski](/wiki/Rados%C5%82aw_Sikorski "Radosław Sikorski") met separately with United States [Secretary of State](/wiki/Secretary_of_State_(United_States) "Secretary of State (United States)") [Marco Rubio](/wiki/Marco_Rubio "Marco Rubio").[[97]](#cite_note-99) On 3 September, Nawrocki arrived to the meeting, where Trump affirmed his intention to maintain the presence of American troops in Poland and invited Nawrocki to the upcoming [G20](/wiki/G20 "G20") summit in [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida").[[98]](#cite_note-100)

In 2025, Nawrocki cancelled a planned meeting with Hungarian Prime Minister [Viktor Orbán](/wiki/Viktor_Orb%C3%A1n "Viktor Orbán"). One of Nawrocki's Chancellery members, [Marcin Przydacz](/wiki/Marcin_Przydacz "Marcin Przydacz"), explained how the president disapproved of Orbán's relationship with Russian President [Vladimir Putin](/wiki/Vladimir_Putin "Vladimir Putin") and the fact that the Hungarian leader had recently visited the Russian president. Przydacz added that president Nawrocki will seek to limit his diplomatic contacts with Orbán solely to [Visegrád Group](/wiki/Visegr%C3%A1d_Group "Visegrád Group") meetings.[[99]](#cite_note-101)[[100]](#cite_note-102)

#### Foreign policy stances toward the European Union

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Foreign policy stances toward the European Union")]

As president, Nawrocki opposes the [EU–Mercosur Association Agreement](/wiki/EU%E2%80%93Mercosur_Association_Agreement "EU–Mercosur Association Agreement"), promising to uphold the slogan "Polish farmer, Polish field, Polish bread on the Polish table" ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *Polski rolnik, polskie pole, polski chleb na polskim stole*).[[101]](#cite_note-103)[[102]](#cite_note-104) Nawrocki opposes the implementation of the EU Digital Services Act as proposed by the Tusk government, with the members of his Chancellery calling the proposed ability of the President of the Office of Electronic Communications to block content "too far-reaching".[[103]](#cite_note-105)

#### Foreign policy stances toward Ukraine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Foreign policy stances toward Ukraine")]

In August 2025, Nawrocki vetoed a bill that would have extended financial support for Ukrainian refugees of the [Russo-Ukrainian war](/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_war "Russo-Ukrainian war") not employed in Poland as well as funding for [Starlink](/wiki/Starlink "Starlink") systems to maintain Ukraine's internet connectivity.[[104]](#cite_note-106) Nawrocki was invited to meet with [president](/wiki/President_of_Ukraine "President of Ukraine") [Volodymyr Zelenskyy](/wiki/Volodymyr_Zelenskyy "Volodymyr Zelenskyy") of [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") in its capital city, [Kyiv](/wiki/Kyiv "Kyiv"), but Nawrocki rejected the invitation, suggesting that Zelenskyy should come to Poland instead.[[105]](#cite_note-107) Nawrocki's response was supported by public opinion.[[106]](#cite_note-108)

Nawrocki is considered a nonpartisan, but with a conservative outlook.[[119]](#cite_note-121) Nawrocki describes himself as a "representative of the broadly defined patriotic camp", and stresses that he has never belonged to a political party.[[120]](#cite_note-kot-122) He considers himself a "citizens' candidate" that will end the "Polish-Polish war".[[121]](#cite_note-123) He declared that he is ready to support "any Polish government that demands the exhumation of [Polish victims in Volhynia](/wiki/Massacres_of_Poles_in_Volhynia_and_Eastern_Galicia "Massacres of Poles in Volhynia and Eastern Galicia")", and describes issues of history and social accountability as his "demarcation lines".[[122]](#cite_note-124) [Euronews](/wiki/Euronews "Euronews") describes the political alignment of Nawrocki's campaign as "patriotic, pro-Christian, pro-NATO and favourable to president [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump")".[[29]](#cite_note-:0-30) The *[Financial Times](/wiki/Financial_Times "Financial Times")* described Nawrocki as "anti-liberal, anti-German, anti-EU".[[123]](#cite_note-125)

### Social issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Social issues")]

Nawrocki is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") with [culturally conservative](/wiki/Cultural_conservatism "Cultural conservatism") views and accused [the ruling coalition](/wiki/15_October_Coalition "15 October Coalition") and [LGBT groups](/wiki/LGBTQ_grooming_conspiracy_theory "LGBTQ grooming conspiracy theory") of "sexualising children", demonstratively throwing a copy of the cover of *[Gender Queer: A Memoir](/wiki/Gender_Queer:_A_Memoir "Gender Queer: A Memoir")* into a [paper shredder](/wiki/Paper_shredder "Paper shredder") during the 2025 campaign.[[124]](#cite_note-BBC-126)[[125]](#cite_note-angryLGBT-127) Nawrocki supports maintaining close ties between the [Catholic Church in Poland](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Poland "Catholic Church in Poland") and the [Polish government](/wiki/Politics_of_Poland "Politics of Poland"), the [broad criminalisation of abortion](/wiki/Abortion_in_Poland "Abortion in Poland"), and opposes the legalisation of [same-sex marriage](/wiki/Same-sex_marriage "Same-sex marriage"), [civil unions](/wiki/Civil_union "Civil union") or extending legal rights to people in same-sex relationships, citing [Catholic sexual ethics](/wiki/Catholic_theology_of_sexuality "Catholic theology of sexuality").[[124]](#cite_note-BBC-126)[[126]](#cite_note-128)

During the campaign, Nawrocki stated: "There is a male sex and a female sex. And if anyone defies that, they don't understand the present, the past, or the future" and "marriage is a union between a man and a woman."[[127]](#cite_note-legrand-129) Nawrocki also opposes euthanasia, and his position on abortion has been described as "even more conservative than that of PiS itself";[[127]](#cite_note-legrand-129) he stated that he would not allow the return of pre-2021 abortion laws in Poland, which allowed abortion in case of serious diseases, arguing: "I would not sign a law that would reinstate the agreement on abortion. As a future president, I cannot allow the abortion of children with [Down syndrome](/wiki/Down_syndrome "Down syndrome")."[[128]](#cite_note-elentir-130)

He stresses his commitment to promote Polish patriotism, Christian values and national sovereignty, and has declared the need to defend traditional social values.[[129]](#cite_note-salon24-131) He has stated his strong opposition to removal of crosses from state buildings.[[130]](#cite_note-132) He holds [anti-communist](/wiki/Anti-communism "Anti-communism") views, and has criticised the Polish education system, claiming that Polish education is controlled by the "post-communist party environment". In February 2024, he was listed as one of the persons wanted by the [Russian Federation](/wiki/Russian_Federation "Russian Federation") on criminal charges for the [removal of monuments](/wiki/List_of_monuments_and_memorials_removed_following_the_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "List of monuments and memorials removed following the Russian invasion of Ukraine") commemorating the [Red Army](/wiki/Red_Army "Red Army") erected in the former [Polish People's Republic](/wiki/Polish_People%27s_Republic "Polish People's Republic") in the years 1944–1989.[[131]](#cite_note-133) He has described [cursed soldiers](/wiki/Cursed_soldiers "Cursed soldiers") as national heroes of Poland and praised Law and Justice for implementing a National Remembrance Day in their honour.[[129]](#cite_note-salon24-131) He declared himself an opponent of the [EU's migration policy](/wiki/Migration_and_asylum_policy_of_the_European_Union "Migration and asylum policy of the European Union") and announced his intention to terminate the EU's [migration pact](/wiki/New_Pact_on_Migration_and_Asylum "New Pact on Migration and Asylum").[[132]](#cite_note-134)

### Economics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Economics")]

Nawrocki's program has been described as [economically interventionist](/wiki/Economic_interventionism "Economic interventionism"),[[133]](#cite_note-135) [economically nationalist](/wiki/Economic_nationalism "Economic nationalism"),[[134]](#cite_note-136) and economically left-wing.[[135]](#cite_note-137) He pledged to implement new policies that will combat "unfair competition" in the Polish economy.[[136]](#cite_note-money-138) Nawrocki describes himself as a strong supporter of armaments and social investment programmes. In a speech in which he accepted Law and Justice's endorsement for his candidacy, Nawrocki pledged to abolish all [overtime](/wiki/Overtime "Overtime") labor tax and to focus on large economic investments; he strongly supports the [Central Communication Port](/wiki/Central_Communication_Port "Central Communication Port") project, and praised the [Central Industrial Region](/wiki/Central_Industrial_Region_(Poland) "Central Industrial Region (Poland)") and [Stocznia Gdynia](/wiki/Stocznia_Gdynia "Stocznia Gdynia") schemes developed in the interwar [Second Polish Republic](/wiki/Second_Polish_Republic "Second Polish Republic").[[137]](#cite_note-onet_eko-139) Nawrocki expressed his fascination with other large investments such as the [Vistula Spit canal](/wiki/Vistula_Spit_canal "Vistula Spit canal") and [Świnoujście LNG terminal](/wiki/%C5%9Awinouj%C5%9Bcie_LNG_terminal "Świnoujście LNG terminal"), and wants to pursue similar undertakings.[[138]](#cite_note-140)

Nawrocki calls for "a welfare state with zero VAT on food".[[139]](#cite_note-141) He promised to increase social spending, welfare benefits and pensions;[[140]](#cite_note-142) he also supports welfare programmes and opposes adopting the [Euro](/wiki/Euro_currency "Euro currency") as Poland's currency.[[141]](#cite_note-143) He stresses the lack of transport in Poland and has pledged to develop rail infrastructure in underdeveloped regions of the country.[[142]](#cite_note-144) Nawrocki proposes to enact an additional tax on the owners of three and more apartments, with an exemption for families with children.[[143]](#cite_note-145) His other proposals include abolishing tax on savings and increasing the annual indexation of pensions.[[144]](#cite_note-Bloomberg-146) He also expressed [welfare chauvinist](/wiki/Welfare_chauvinism "Welfare chauvinism") views - believing that Poles are "treated worse in their own country than immigrants", Nawrocki argues that social benefits in Poland should be for Poles only, and pensions for Ukrainian immigrants should be eliminated; additionally, Polish citizens should have a priority in healthcare, school and kindergarten queues.[[145]](#cite_note-147)[[146]](#cite_note-148) Nawrocki is also considered to align with "Trumpian tariffs and [protectionism](/wiki/Protectionism "Protectionism")".[[147]](#cite_note-149)

He objected to the [Tusk government](/wiki/Third_Cabinet_of_Donald_Tusk "Third Cabinet of Donald Tusk")'s cut in healthcare contributions for businesses and stated that he opposes any attempt to reduce [healthcare funding](/wiki/Health_care_in_Poland "Health care in Poland").[[148]](#cite_note-150) Nawrocki also signed a list of 11 pledges, including promises to not raise the retirement age, and to uphold worker protections, defend minimum wages, retain the ban on Sunday business activity, to promote [economic patriotism](/wiki/Economic_patriotism "Economic patriotism"), and to increase funding for the public health service and [agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture_in_Poland "Agriculture in Poland"). Based on these pledges, he was endorsed by Poland's [Solidarity](/wiki/Solidarity_(Polish_trade_union) "Solidarity (Polish trade union)") trade union.[[149]](#cite_note-151) He supports additional taxes on "digital giants operating online and targeting Polish users" and the expansion of [social housing](/wiki/Social_housing "Social housing").[[150]](#cite_note-152)

He argues that Poland needs to achieve "full energy sovereignty". He supports [nuclear energy](/wiki/Nuclear_power "Nuclear power"), describing it as "the most secure and stable one". He also criticised the [European Green Deal](/wiki/European_Green_Deal "European Green Deal"), stating that while he supports environmental protection, he opposes "climate madness at the expense of Polish homes, workers and entrepreneurs". Nawrocki also believes that Poland needs to ensure its food security and respect "the sovereignty of the Polish countryside"; he has described rural Poland as "the mainstay of Polish culture, traditions and social values".[[137]](#cite_note-onet_eko-139) He supports selective tax cuts, but is considered to not be [economically liberal](/wiki/Economically_liberal "Economically liberal").[[128]](#cite_note-elentir-130) He advocated for reducing the [value-added tax](/wiki/Value-added_tax "Value-added tax") rate from 23% to 22% during the 2025 campaign, as well as an exemption from [personal income tax](/wiki/Personal_income_tax "Personal income tax") for families with two or more children. He also pledged to veto blanket tax increases.[[144]](#cite_note-Bloomberg-146)[[151]](#cite_note-153)

### Foreign policy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Foreign policy")]

Karol Nawrocki has emphasised in his statements that the geopolitical interest of the Polish state lies in pushing back the [Russian Federation](/wiki/Russian_Federation "Russian Federation").[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) He has stated that "Russia is imperialist in its foundation whether it is [white terror](/wiki/White_Terror_(Russia) "White Terror (Russia)"), [red terror](/wiki/Red_terror "Red terror") or modern terror".[[152]](#cite_note-wm.pl-nawrocki-154) According to him, the foundation of Poland's security is a strong position within [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") and a close alliance with the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) He also supports regional alliances such as the [Bucharest Nine](/wiki/Bucharest_Nine "Bucharest Nine") and favours expanding this format to include [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") and [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland").[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) Nawrocki is an advocate of increasing defense spending and expanding the army to 300,000 troops. However, he opposes conscription or mandatory military training for young men.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2)

Geopolitically, Nawrocki supports further [strengthening Polish ties to the United States](/wiki/Poland%E2%80%93United_States_relations "Poland–United States relations") and [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") while opposing [European integration](/wiki/European_integration "European integration").[[29]](#cite_note-:0-30) He opposes the [federalisation of the European Union](/wiki/Federalisation_of_the_European_Union "Federalisation of the European Union") and stresses the need to maintain Polish national identity within the European Union, adding that "Poland does not need a centralised state populated by EU citizens of Polish origin".[[136]](#cite_note-money-138) Nawrocki criticised what he considered to be the overly submissive policy of [Donald Tusk's government](/wiki/Third_Cabinet_of_Donald_Tusk "Third Cabinet of Donald Tusk") toward Germany. He advocated for the restoration of a balanced and partnership-based relationship between the two countries.[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) He also demands [World War II reparations](/wiki/World_War_II_reparations "World War II reparations") from Germany for [material losses](/wiki/Polish_material_losses_during_World_War_II "Polish material losses during World War II") and [war crimes](/wiki/Nazi_war_crimes_in_occupied_Poland_during_World_War_II "Nazi war crimes in occupied Poland during World War II") committed in [occupied Poland](/wiki/Occupation_of_Poland_(1939%E2%80%931945) "Occupation of Poland (1939–1945)").[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/DHS_Secretary_Kristi_Noem_Travels_to_Poland_-_54553881558.jpg/250px-DHS_Secretary_Kristi_Noem_Travels_to_Poland_-_54553881558.jpg)](/wiki/File:DHS_Secretary_Kristi_Noem_Travels_to_Poland_-_54553881558.jpg)

Nawrocki with [DHS](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Homeland_Security "United States Department of Homeland Security") Secretary [Kristi Noem](/wiki/Kristi_Noem "Kristi Noem"), May 2025

He supports ending the [Russo-Ukrainian war](/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_war "Russo-Ukrainian war") by a [peace agreement](/wiki/Peace_negotiations_in_the_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Peace negotiations in the Russian invasion of Ukraine") but argues that the issue of territorial cessions should be decided by the European community as well as Ukraine itself.[[152]](#cite_note-wm.pl-nawrocki-154) Nawrocki opposed the deployment of Polish troops to Ukraine. He supported the normalisation of relations with Ukraine on a basis of partnership, as well as the recognition and dignified burial of Polish victims in [Volhynia](/wiki/Massacres_of_Poles_in_Volhynia_and_Eastern_Galicia "Massacres of Poles in Volhynia and Eastern Galicia").[[1]](#cite_note-:1-2) Nawrocki is opposed to Ukrainian membership in [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") or the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") until Ukraine accepts responsibility for the genocide of Poles in Volhynia. He has previously denounced attempts to downplay the 1943–1945 [massacres of Poles in Volhynia and Eastern Galicia](/wiki/Massacres_of_Poles_in_Volhynia_and_Eastern_Galicia "Massacres of Poles in Volhynia and Eastern Galicia") for the sake of improving [Polish-Ukrainian relations](/wiki/Polish-Ukrainian_relations "Polish-Ukrainian relations").[[153]](#cite_note-155) He would seek to punish the glorification of Ukrainian nationalist leader [Stepan Bandera](/wiki/Stepan_Bandera "Stepan Bandera").[[154]](#cite_note-156) Nawrocki distanced himself from the former pro-Ukrainian policy of PiS and criticized Ukraine for acting against Polish interests.[[155]](#cite_note-157) He was accused of anti-Ukrainian sentiment,[[156]](#cite_note-158) and "echoing Kremlin talking points about Ukraine".[[157]](#cite_note-159) Responding to the accusations of speaking "Putin's language" on Ukraine, Nawrocki stated: "I speak the language of Polish people. Millions want to say that Zelensky mistreats us, but they're silenced with 'you're spreading Putin's propaganda'."[[158]](#cite_note-160)

He was criticised in [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") for downplaying the role of Poles in [the Holocaust](/wiki/The_Holocaust "The Holocaust").[[159]](#cite_note-161) Nawrocki said he would defend Poland "against all disgusting attacks" by Holocaust scholars.[[160]](#cite_note-162) Poland's foreign minister, [Radosław Sikorski](/wiki/Rados%C5%82aw_Sikorski "Radosław Sikorski"), publicly defended Nawrocki in response to the criticism.[[161]](#cite_note-163) During the election campaign, Nawrocki defended the [2018 amendment](/wiki/Amendment_to_the_Act_on_the_Institute_of_National_Remembrance "Amendment to the Act on the Institute of National Remembrance") that criminalized attributing responsibility for the Holocaust to the Polish nation, stating: "I am fighting for this truth. Poles are not antisemites. Many Poles lost their lives during World War II saving Jews." When asked about Israel, Nawrocki expressed his belief that "there is no state that is a chosen nation to live out its history".[[162]](#cite_note-164) He also promised to end the tradition of lighting [Hanukkah candles](/wiki/Hanukkah_menorah "Hanukkah menorah") in the Presidential Palace,[[163]](#cite_note-165) stating that commitment to Christian values would exclude celebrating Jewish holidays.[[127]](#cite_note-legrand-129)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Karol Nawrocki is married to [Marta Nawrocka](/wiki/Marta_Nawrocka "Marta Nawrocka") (born in 1986), a graduate of the Faculty of Law and Administration at the [University of Gdańsk](/wiki/University_of_Gda%C5%84sk "University of Gdańsk") and an employee of the National Revenue Administration.[[164]](#cite_note-:2-166) She specialises in the control of the oil industry and combating illegal trade. Together, they have two children: a son Antoni and a daughter, Katarzyna. They also raised Daniel (born in 2003), Marta Nawrocka's son from a previous relationship.[[164]](#cite_note-:2-166) The couple married in 2010, and Karol Nawrocki adopted Daniel.[[164]](#cite_note-:2-166) The pair signed their son, Antoni, out of "health education" classes introduced by the [Ministry of National Education](/wiki/Ministry_of_National_Education_(Poland) "Ministry of National Education (Poland)") under [Barbara Nowacka](/wiki/Barbara_Nowacka "Barbara Nowacka").[[165]](#cite_note-167) After Nawrocki's election in 2025, he initially moved into the [Belweder](/wiki/Belweder "Belweder"), but in October of the same year moved to the [Presidential Palace](/wiki/Presidential_Palace,_Warsaw "Presidential Palace, Warsaw").[[166]](#cite_note-168)

Daniel Nawrocki studies at the Faculty of Law and Administration at the University of Gdańsk. He is involved in journalism, working for Gazeta Morska and previously for Dziennik Bałtycki. In 2023, he was appointed a member of the Youth Sports Council under the Minister of Sport. He was also a member of the Youth Council of the City of Gdańsk and now serves as a neighborhood councilor. In 2024, he ran unsuccessfully for a seat on the [Gdańsk City Council](/wiki/Gda%C5%84sk_City_Council "Gdańsk City Council") as a candidate of the Law and Justice party.[[167]](#cite_note-169)

### Public approval

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Public approval")]

See also: [Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency § Approval rating](/wiki/Opinion_polling_on_the_Karol_Nawrocki_presidency#Approval_rating "Opinion polling on the Karol Nawrocki presidency")

During the first months of Nawrocki's presidency, he maintained a positive approval rating, being one of Poland's most approved politicians. His popularity was highest among Polish youth, with a United Surveys poll showing 73% approval among respondents aged 18–29, and lowest among those over 70, where approval stood at 42%.[[168]](#cite_note-170)

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Sports")]

Nawrocki's image is tied to sports, especially [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"). During the early stages of [his 2025 presidential campaign](/wiki/2025_Polish_presidential_election#Nawrocki's_campaign "2025 Polish presidential election"), Nawrocki appeared in workout videos,[[169]](#cite_note-171) and compared himself to *[Rocky](/wiki/Rocky_(franchise) "Rocky (franchise)")*, with his campaign [merchandise](/wiki/Merchandise "Merchandise") portraying catchphrase "Now Rocky", a play on his surname.[[170]](#cite_note-172) Nawrocki's participation in a [football hooligans' fight](/wiki/Football_hooliganism_in_Poland "Football hooliganism in Poland") ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *ustawka*) between fans of [Lechia Gdańsk](/wiki/Lechia_Gda%C5%84sk "Lechia Gdańsk") and [Lech Poznań](/wiki/Lech_Pozna%C5%84 "Lech Poznań") was controversial, attracting praise[[171]](#cite_note-173)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] and criticism.[[172]](#cite_note-174)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] Since his inauguration, Nawrocki maintained his athletic and [football-fan](/wiki/Association_football_culture "Association football culture") image, seen working out[[173]](#cite_note-175) or attending football matches in Poland and on foreign visits.[[174]](#cite_note-176) *[Polityka](/wiki/Polityka "Polityka")* argues Nawrocki wants to be a "patron of football fans".[[175]](#cite_note-177)

### Public engagement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Public engagement")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d3/Warsaw_Polish_Armed_Forces_Day_parade_%289262161%29.jpg/250px-Warsaw_Polish_Armed_Forces_Day_parade_%289262161%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Warsaw_Polish_Armed_Forces_Day_parade_(9262161).jpg)

Karol Nawrocki at [Polish Armed Forces Day](/wiki/Armed_Forces_Day_(Poland) "Armed Forces Day (Poland)") on 15 August 2025

Nawrocki's image as president has been compared to [President of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump").[[176]](#cite_note-178) Nawrocki was described as a president close to the people, a "people's president" or [tribune of the people](/wiki/Tribune_of_the_plebs "Tribune of the plebs") ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *trybun ludowy*). He was also applauded for actively representing Poland in [international visits](/wiki/State_visit "State visit").[[177]](#cite_note-peoplespresident-179) Besides exercising in public, Nawrocki also appeared in public with various members of the public, including eating a [kebab](/wiki/Kebab "Kebab") with youth from a remote village (something he reportedly paid for with his own money) as part of a casual campaign promise[[178]](#cite_note-180) and speaking with Polish citizens who called out for him during a visit to the United States.[[179]](#cite_note-181)

During his 2025 presidential campaign, Nawrocki portrayed himself as a nonpartisan "citizens' candidate" ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *kandydat obywatelski*),[[180]](#cite_note-182) and as an authentic "flesh and bone man" ([Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"): *człowiek z krwi i kości*).[[181]](#cite_note-183)

### Independence March appearance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Karol_Nawrocki&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Independence March appearance")]

Nawrocki appeared at the 2025 [Independence March](/wiki/Independence_March_(Poland) "Independence March (Poland)") on 11 November.[[182]](#cite_note-184)
